product.name: ArWoWear T31 Flame Pro
product.familyDescriptorPl: Trudnopalne z ochroną przed łukiem elektrycznym
product.categorySourceGroup: Flame Retardant, Vat Dyed, Workwear Fabrics Resistant up to 100 Wash
product.titleDescriptor: 90% Cotton 10% Polyamide, Flame Retardant Fabric, 330 gr/m'2
product.specLine: 330 g/m², 90% Cotton 10% Polyamide, VAT Dyed, Flame Retardant (100 Wash Resistant), 3/1S Twill Fabric
descriptions: ArWoWear T31 Flame Pro is heavy weight 330 g/m², 90% cotton 10% polyamide, vat dyed, flame retardant workwear fabric.
It has flame retardant function with EN ISO 11612 and EN ISO 11611 certification.
It is durable up to 100 washing.
90% Cotton 10% Polyamide, Flame Retardant Fabric, 330 gr/m'2
330 g/m², 90% Cotton 10% Polyamide, VAT Dyed, Flame Retardant (100 Wash Resistant), 3/1S Twill Fabric
technicalParameters: Tensile Strength | Warp>1400N; Weft>1200N | EN ISO 13934-1
Tear Strength | Warp>30N; Weft>25N | EN ISO 13937-2
Dimensional Change | -3% < Length < +3% ; -3% < Width < +3% | EN ISO 5077
Dry Cleaning Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 D01
Hot Press Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 X11
Perspiration Light Fast. | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 B07
Azo Test | No Azo Colorants. | EN ISO 14362-1
Flame and Heat Protection | Yes | EN ISO 11612

acceptedMatches.rationale: Explicit EN ISO 11611 welding protection certification and molten-metal splash property directly support welding/metalworking application.

possibleButNotAccepted.reason: Explicit electric-arc protection rating (EN 61482, ATPV) directly ties the fabric to protection from electrical hazards typical of energy-sector work.
EN ISO 11612 flame and heat protection is present, but no explicit firefighter/turnout-gear use is stated; FR alone does not establish firefighting sector.
No explicit military, police or tactical use stated; only generic FR workwear terminology is given.

primary.rationale: Explicit EN ISO 11611 welding protection certification and molten-metal splash resistance make welding/metalworking the most direct and specific stated application.

secondary.rationale: Explicit electric-arc protection rating with ATPV value directly supports independent use in electrical/energy sector hazard protection, separate from welding certification.

alternativesConsidered.reasonNotSelected: Generic FR workwear category exists, but explicit welding and arc certifications indicate more specific sectors.
EN ISO 11612 flame/heat protection alone does not establish firefighter turnout gear use; no explicit firefighting statement.
No explicit military or uniformed-service use stated.
